perturbation.original_text: Rogers shall pay to Licensor a fee (the "License Fee"), which shall be equal to fifty (50)% of Retail Revenues. For the purposes of this Agreement, "Retail Revenues" means the retail revenues actually received by Rogers for each authorized exhibition of a Licensed Program on the ROD Service, less GST and applicable sales tax.
perturbation.changed_text: Rogers shall pay to Licensor a fee (the "License Fee"), which shall be equal to fifty (50)% of Retail Revenues. For the purposes of this Agreement, "Retail Revenues" means the gross retail revenues before any deductions, received by Rogers for each authorized exhibition of a Licensed Program on the ROD Service, including GST and applicable sales tax.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction in the definition of 'Retail Revenues'. Initially, it was defined as revenues 'less GST and applicable sales tax,' but now it's defined as revenues 'including GST and applicable sales tax'. This creates ambiguity in calculating the License Fee, because it is contradicting the previous definition of retail revenue, creating uncertainty in the payment structure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Rogers shall pay to Licensor a fee (the "License Fee"), which shall be equal to fifty (50)% of Retail Revenues. For the purposes of this Agreement, "Retail Revenues" means the retail revenues actually received by Rogers for each authorized exhibition of a Licensed Program on the ROD Service, less GST and applicable sales tax. For greater certainty, Retail Revenues shall not include any fees payable to Rogers for: (i) the purchase, rental or installation of a digital terminal; (ii) any basic or premium television services; or (iii) digital cable access.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original definition of 'Retail Revenues' by including GST and applicable sales tax instead of excluding them. This creates a significant ambiguity in calculating the License Fee, because the contradicted text clearly states Retail Revenues means the revenues actually received less GST and sales tax, while the changed text alters this definition, causing a direct contradiction and uncertainty in the payment structure, as per the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: During the Term, Rogers shall have the non-exclusive right to distribute and exhibit each Licensed Program on a VOD basis for a period of ninety (90) consecutive days, or such longer period as may be agreed to by Rogers and Licensor (the "License Period").
perturbation.changed_text: During the Term, Rogers shall have the non-exclusive right to distribute and exhibit each Licensed Program on a VOD basis for a period of thirty (30) consecutive days, unless otherwise notified and consented in writing by Licensor, or such longer period as may be agreed to by Rogers and Licensor (the "License Period").
perturbation.explanation: The change modifies the License Period from 'ninety (90) consecutive days' to 'thirty (30) consecutive days'. In section 7, the term of the agreement is defined as of the Effective Date and, unless earlier terminated in accordance with this Agreement, shall terminate on June 30, 2010. By reducing the license period, Rogers has less time to exhibit the program within that period, which is contradicting with the original agreement with the Licensor for the distribution of the program. The change to thirty (30) days, creating an intext contradiction if Rogers exhibits outside of the thirty days.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement (the "Initial Term") shall commence as of the Effective Date and, unless earlier terminated in accordance with this Agreement, shall terminate on June 30, 2010. At Rogers' option, this Agreement shall renew for a subsequent term of two (2) years on the terms and conditions herein (the "Renewal Term"). Notwithstanding the foregoing, if, at the expiry of this Agreement following the Initial Term or the Renewal Term (if any), as applicable, Licensor and Rogers have not executed a new agreement governing the VOD distribution and exhibition of Licensed Programs and Rogers (or its permitted assigns) continues to distribute and exhibit Licensed Programs on the ROD Service following such expiry, such continued distribution and exhibition shall be governed by the terms of this Agreement in effect at the time of expiry, except that each of Licensor and Rogers shall have the right, on sixty (60) days' prior written notice, to terminate this Agreement, as so extended. For greater certainty, all references to "Term" in this Agreement shall include the Initial Term, the Renewal Term (if any) and any period during which this Agreement continues following its expiry in accordance with this Section.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'ninety (90) consecutive days' to 'thirty (30) consecutive days' for the VOD distribution period directly contradicts the overall term of the agreement ending on June 30, 2010. If Rogers' license period is reduced to 30 days, but the agreement extends beyond that, it creates an in-text contradiction regarding Rogers' rights and obligations during the remaining term. This inconsistency leads to uncertainty about whether Rogers can legally exhibit the program, conflicting with the agreement's stipulated term for distribution as defined in the contradicted section. The term of the agreement ('Initial Term') shall commence as of the Effective Date and, unless earlier terminated in accordance with this Agreement, shall terminate on June 30, 2010.

perturbation.original_text: Licensor shall, at its sole expense, encode each Licensed Program selected for distribution and exhibition by Rogers hereunder and shall deliver such fully encoded Licensed Program to Rogers on a DLT master tape, DVD-R disc or other master pre-recorded format acceptable to Rogers (each, a "Master") at least thirty (30) days prior to the availability date for such Licensed Program.
perturbation.changed_text: Licensor shall, at Rogers expense, encode each Licensed Program selected for distribution and exhibition by Rogers hereunder and shall deliver such fully encoded Licensed Program to Rogers on a DLT master tape, DVD-R disc or other master pre-recorded format acceptable to Rogers (each, a "Master") at least ten (10) days prior to the availability date for such Licensed Program.
perturbation.explanation: The change shifts the expense of encoding from the Licensor to Rogers. The modification of the time frame for delivery of the encoded program is reduced from thirty (30) days to ten (10) days, prior to the availability date. This now creates a contradiction in 8(b) because, if Rogers is unable to distribute and/or exhibit the Licensed Program on the availability date as a result of the defectiveness of any such Master, the License Fee payable in respect of such Licensed Program shall be reduced by 10% for the balance of the License Period. But this is now on Rogers for not providing the License Program, when previously this was the sole expense of the Licensor and the time frame was 30 days to correct any issues.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) To the extent any Master delivered by Licensor hereunder is determined by Rogers, in its sole discretion, acting reasonably, to be defective: (i) Licensor shall forthwith provide Rogers with a replacement Master; and (ii) if Rogers is unable to distribute and/or exhibit the Licensed Program on the availability date as a result of the defectiveness of any such Master, the License Fee payable in respect of such Licensed Program shall be reduced by 10% for the balance of the License Period.
perturbation.justification: The modified text shifts the expense of encoding from the Licensor to Rogers and reduces the delivery timeframe from 30 to 10 days. This directly contradicts the original intention of section (b), which holds the Licensor responsible for delivering a non-defective master. Now, with Rogers bearing the encoding expense and having less time to address defects, the 10% reduction in the license fee due to a defective master effectively penalizes Rogers for an issue they are now responsible for and may not have adequate time to resolve, thus creating a clear in-text contradiction and uncertainty around responsibility, and fits the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
